title: Harvest Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Harvest** is a time-tracking + invoicing SaaS exposing a REST API under `https://api.harvestapp.com/v2`. This connector — `HarvestConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "harvest"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from a Harvest account:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Time Entries | `/time_entries` | List, get, create, update, delete (the core Harvest signal) |
| Projects | `/projects` | List + read active projects |
| Tasks | `/tasks` | List task definitions |
| Clients | `/clients` | List + read clients (engagements) |
| Users | `/users`, `/users/me` | List users; identify the authenticated user |
| Invoices | `/invoices` | List invoices (drafts / open / paid / closed) |
| Estimates | `/estimates` | List estimates / quotes |
| Expenses | `/expenses` | List logged expenses |

The connector normalises time entries + invoices + clients into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::HarvestHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate a Harvest Personal Access Token

1. Sign in to https://id.getharvest.com/developers.
2. Click **Create New Personal Access Token**.
3. Name it (e.g. `Shielva ACP`) and choose the Harvest account it should authorise.
4. Copy the **token value** — Harvest shows it only once.
5. Copy the **Account ID** from the same page (numeric, e.g. `1234567`).

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Harvest**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `access_token` (secret) | PAT from step 4 |
| `account_id` (text) | Numeric Account ID |
| `user_agent` (text, optional) | Defaults to `Shielva Harvest Connector (support@shielva.ai)` |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://api.harvestapp.com/v2` (rarely needed) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 100 (Harvest's documented limit) |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `access_token` + `account_id` are present (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `HarvestHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which fetches `GET /users/me` as a lightweight authenticated probe. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 403 → `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Harvest REST API uses **Personal Access Token + Account ID** for server-to-server access (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry — PATs are revoked manually.

### Credential model

- `access_token` is sent in the `Authorization` header as `Bearer <token>`.
- `account_id` is sent in the `Harvest-Account-Id` header (Harvest's per-account routing).
- `user_agent` is sent in the `User-Agent` header — **Harvest requires this** and recommends naming the integration plus a contact address.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `access_token` + `account_id`. Initialises `HarvestHTTPClient`. Does **not** call any Harvest endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a synthetic `TokenInfo` carrying the PAT — there is no token exchange. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /users/me` — minimal authenticated probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — PATs do not expire. |
| token storage | None — the PAT lives in the install config. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Authorization:       Bearer <access_token>
Harvest-Account-Id:  <account_id>
User-Agent:          <user_agent>
Content-Type:        application/json
Accept:              application/json
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `access_token` | secret | yes | `self.access_token` | `Authorization: Bearer …` header value |
| `account_id` | text | yes | `self.account_id` | `Harvest-Account-Id` header |
| `user_agent` | text | no | `self.user_agent` (default `Shielva Harvest Connector (support@shielva.ai)`) | `User-Agent` header (required by Harvest) |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://api.harvestapp.com/v2`) | Override for sandbox / proxy |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 100) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["access_token", "account_id"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_HARVEST_BASE = "https://api.harvestapp.com/v2"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `HarvestConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::HarvestHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `access_token` + `account_id`; initialise `HarvestHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — returns a `TokenInfo` carrying the PAT for ABI compatibility.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Fetches `GET /users/me` as a lightweight authenticated probe. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates Harvest `/time_entries`, `/invoices`, `/clients` → `NormalizedDocument` → `ingest_document`. Walks `next_page` until exhausted. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Users
children.content:
### `async get_user_me() -> Dict`
`GET /users/me` — authenticated user.

### `async list_users(*, is_active=True, page=1, per_page=100) -> Dict`
`GET /users` — paginated user list.

children.title: Clients · Projects · Tasks
children.content:
### `async list_clients(*, is_active=True, page=1, per_page=100) -> Dict`
`GET /clients`.

### `async list_projects(*, is_active=True, client_id=None, page=1, per_page=100) -> Dict`
`GET /projects` — optionally scoped to a client.

### `async get_project(project_id) -> Dict`
`GET /projects/{project_id}`.

### `async list_tasks(*, is_active=True, page=1, per_page=100) -> Dict`
`GET /tasks` — task definitions.

children.title: Time Entries
children.content:
### `async list_time_entries(*, from_date=None, to_date=None, user_id=None, project_id=None, client_id=None, is_billed=None, page=1, per_page=100) -> Dict`
`GET /time_entries` — filter by date range, user, project, client, or billed state. Date params are coerced to `YYYY-MM-DD` via `iso_date()`.

### `async get_time_entry(time_entry_id) -> Dict`
`GET /time_entries/{id}`.

### `async create_time_entry(project_id, task_id, spent_date, *, hours=None, notes=None, user_id=None) -> Dict`
`POST /time_entries` — body: `{project_id, task_id, spent_date, hours?, notes?, user_id?}`.

### `async update_time_entry(time_entry_id, fields) -> Dict`
`PATCH /time_entries/{id}` — arbitrary field map.

### `async delete_time_entry(time_entry_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /time_entries/{id}`.

children.title: Invoices · Estimates · Expenses
children.content:
### `async list_invoices(*, state=None, client_id=None, page=1, per_page=100) -> Dict`
`GET /invoices` — filter by state (draft/open/paid/closed) or client.

### `async list_estimates(*, state=None, client_id=None, page=1, per_page=100) -> Dict`
`GET /estimates`.

### `async list_expenses(*, from_date=None, to_date=None, user_id=None, project_id=None, page=1, per_page=100) -> Dict`
`GET /expenses` — paginated expenses, optional date range / scope.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
HarvestError                          # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── HarvestAuthError                  # 401 / 403
├── HarvestBadRequestError            # 400 / 422
├── HarvestNotFoundError              # 404
├── HarvestConflictError              # 409
├── HarvestRateLimitError             # 429 — retry_after_s
└── HarvestServerError                # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
HarvestNetworkError = HarvestServerError    # legacy name from older code
HarvestNotFound     = HarvestNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 422 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `_BACKOFF_BASE * 2 ** attempt` (0.5s, 1s, 2s), retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3`. Honours `Retry-After` header if present. |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `HarvestNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)     — logged as warning
other → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `access_token` or `account_id` blank | Re-enter both in ACP. Harvest shows the PAT only once at generation — rotate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 from Harvest — PAT revoked or wrong account_id | Verify `account_id` matches the account that owns the PAT. Rotate the PAT if needed. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 — PAT lacks scope on this account | Recreate the PAT scoped to the correct Harvest account. |
| `harvest.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Harvest's 100 req/min cap) | The client retries with exponential backoff. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer requests. |
| 422 on `create_time_entry` | Validation — missing `project_id`/`task_id` or invalid `spent_date` | `iso_date()` accepts `YYYY-MM-DD`, `date`, or `datetime`. Ensure `project_id` and `task_id` are integers from `list_projects` / `list_tasks`. |
| `User-Agent` warnings in Harvest support emails | Default UA was overridden with an empty string | Leave `user_agent` blank to use the Shielva default, or set a string that names your integration + a contact email. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'HarvestNetworkError'` | Older exceptions.py without back-compat aliases | Already fixed — `HarvestNetworkError = HarvestServerError` alias added in `exceptions.py`. |
